patterns.description: rm -rf on paths outside /tmp or project directory
patterns.safer_alternative: Use trash-cli (trash-put) or move to a backup directory instead of permanent deletion
Use rm (reversible from backups) or move to a staging directory for review before deletion

patterns.description: dd writing zeros or random data to a device or file
patterns.safer_alternative: Verify the target path carefully. Use truncate for file zeroing or fallocate for space allocation
Use chmod 755 for directories or chmod 644 for files. Only grant the minimum permissions needed
List contents first with ls, then delete specific files. Or use trash-cli for recoverable deletion
Run find without -delete first to preview matches, then add -delete after confirming
Back up the file first (cp file file.bak), then truncate
Copy first (cp), verify the copy, then remove the original

patterns.description: Copying files that may overwrite existing files without backup
patterns.safer_alternative: Use cp --backup=numbered to preserve existing files
